audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for arrest, council, name/saving-healing witness, boldness, no other name, prayer with Psalm 2, Spirit-filled speech, common possessions, Barnabas.

verses.restored: As they were speaking to the people, the priests, captain of the temple, and Sadducees came upon them,
being greatly troubled because they were teaching the people and announcing in Jesus the resurrection from the dead.
They laid hands on them and put them in custody until the next day, for it was already evening.
But many of those who heard the word trusted, and the number of the men came to about five thousand.
It happened on the next day that their rulers, elders, and scribes were gathered in Jerusalem,
with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and as many as were from the high-priestly family.
Setting them in the middle, they were inquiring, In what power or in what name did you do this?
Then Peter, filled with holy Spirit, said to them, Rulers of the people and elders,
if we today are examined concerning a good deed to a weak human, by what this one has been saved/healed,

let it be known to all of you and to all the people of Israel that in the name of Jesus Messiah the Nazarene, whom you crucified, whom the Divine raised from the dead, in this one this human stands before you whole.
This is the stone treated as nothing by you the builders, which became head of the corner.
And there is saving/healing in no other, for there is no other name under heaven given among humans by which it is necessary for us to be saved/healed.
Beholding the boldness of Peter and John and perceiving that they were unlettered and ordinary humans, they marveled; and they recognized them as those who had been with Jesus.
Seeing the human who had been healed standing with them, they had nothing to say against it.
Commanding them to go outside the council, they were conferring with one another,
saying, What shall we do with these humans? For that a known sign has happened through them is manifest to all those dwelling in Jerusalem, and we are not able to deny it.
But so that it may not spread further among the people, let us threaten them not to speak anymore in this name to any human.
Calling them, they commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
But Peter and John answered and said to them, Whether it is righteous before the Divine to hear you rather than the Divine, judge.
For we are not able not to speak of what we saw and heard.
After threatening them further, they released them, finding nothing for which they might punish them, because of the people, for all were glorifying the Divine over what had happened.
For the human on whom this sign of healing had happened was more than forty years old.
Having been released, they came to their own and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said to them.
Hearing, they lifted one voice to the Divine and said, Master, you who made heaven and earth and sea and all things in them,
who through holy Spirit by the mouth of our father David your servant said, Why did nations rage and peoples meditate empty things?
The kings of the earth stood near, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord and against his Messiah.
For in truth, in this city, they were gathered against your holy servant/child Jesus, whom you anointed: both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with nations and peoples of Israel,
to do whatever your hand and your purpose had predestined to happen.
And now, Lord, look upon their threats and give to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,
while you stretch out your hand for healing, and signs and wonders happen through the name of your holy servant/child Jesus.
When they had prayed, the place in which they were gathered was shaken, and they were all filled with holy Spirit and were speaking the word of the Divine with boldness.
The multitude of those who trusted were one heart and soul/life, and not one said that anything belonging to him was his own, but all things were common to them.
With great power the apostles were giving witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.
For there was not anyone needy among them; as many as were owners of lands or houses were selling and bringing the proceeds of what was sold,
and laying them at the apostles' feet; it was distributed to each as anyone had need.
Joseph, who was called Barnabas by the apostles, which is translated Son of Encouragement, a Levite, Cypriot by birth,
having a field, sold it, brought the silver, and laid it at the apostles' feet.
